data.title: Texmaco Rail & Engineering's CFO Resigns
data.description: Texmaco Rail & Engineering said its chief financial officer, Hemant Bhuwania, has resigned from the company with effect from March 31, to pursue other opportunities, according to a Wednesday filing to the Indian stock exchanges.

data.title: Market Chatter: Naver Founder Lee Expected to Return as Board Chairman Amid Rising AI Competition
data.description:
Lee Hae-jin, Naver's founder, is poised to return as chairman of the board after a seven-year hiatus, Business Korea reported Wednesday.

Lee had stepped down in 2018 to focus on global expansion efforts. His return comes as competition in the AI industry escalates, highlighted by Chinese startup DeepSeek's launch of a model rivaling OpenAI, the report said.

Naver's board is set to review a proposal this week to appoint Lee, currently the Global Investment Officer, as an internal director and likely board chairman, the report said, citing industry sources.

Shares of Naver fell nearly 1% in recent trade on Thursday.

(Market Chatter news is derived from conversations with market professionals globally. This information is believed to be from reliable sources but may include rumor and speculation. Accuracy is not guaranteed.)

data.title: Beijing Biostar Pharmaceuticals Cancer Drug Receives US FDA Approval
data.description:
Beijing Biostar Pharmaceuticals received approval from the U.S. Food and Drug Administration (FDA) for its international multi-center Phase II/III registration clinical study for Utidelone Capsules, according to a Thursday filing with the Hong Kong bourse.

UTD2 with capecitabine and oxaliplatin for first-line treatment of advanced or metastatic gastric cancer in PD-L1-negative patients, the filing added.

data.title: KBC Global Gets Board Nod to Change Company's Name
data.description: Real estate developer KBC Global has received board approval to change the company's name to Universal JA EPC Infra Limited or any other name as approved by Registrar of Companies, India, according to a Wednesday filing to the Indian stock exchanges.

data.title: Copper Rises as Investors Monitor Impact of U.S.-China Trade War — Market Talk
data.description: Copper prices are higher in early Asian trade as investors closely monitored the impact of the U.S.-China trade war. After Chinese markets returned from the Lunar New Year holiday, investors appear to "take the rising trade tensions in their stride, with market activity relatively subdued," ANZ Research analysts say in a note. Investors are in search for any signs of a rebound in demand following the holidays amid a rise in supply, they add. The benchmark three-month LME contract is up 1.0% at $9,332.00 a ton. (sherry.qin@wsj.com)

data.title: Nomura is on the cusp of its promised re-invention
data.description:
A strong performance by its investment bank helped the $20 bln Japanese firm deliver an 11.8% return on equity last quarter. Profit is more diversified than when Kentaro Okuda took the reins five years ago. His next job is to convince investors that the results are sustainable.

Full view will be published shortly.

Follow @ugalani on X

CONTEXT NEWS

Nomura, Japan's biggest brokerage and investment bank, on February 5 reported third-quarter net profit of 101.4 billion yen ($662 million) for the three months to the end of December, a 101% increase from the same period in the previous year. The results beat the mean estimate of analysts compiled by Refinitiv.

Net revenue rose 25% over the same period to 502 billion yen. Nomura’s shares rose nearly 7% to 1,068 yen apiece in morning trading on February 6.

data.title: Whitebark Energy to Raise AU$2 Million Ahead of King Energy Takeover, Shares Down 13%
data.description:
Whitebark Energy is set to raise AU$2 million by placing around 307.7 million shares at AU$0.0065 apiece, according to a Thursday filing with the Australian bourse.

Participants will also receive one free attached option per two shares subscribed exercisable at AU$0.015 expiring 24 months from the date of issue, the filing said.

Tranche one of the placement will be issued under bourse rule 7.1 for 37.1 million shares and rule 7.1a for 24.8 million shares, while tranche two for 245.8 million shares will be subject to shareholder approval.

The company will use the raised funds to cover the costs of its offer to acquire King Energy, among other initiatives, the filing added.

It filed a bidder's statement to acquire King Energy via an off-market takeover, per the filing.

Peak Asset Management and AE Advisors were the joint lead managers for the placement.

Company shares were down 13% in recent trade.

data.title: China Stocks Rise on AI Optimism
data.description:
The Shanghai Composite rose 0.2% to around 3,235 while the Shenzhen Component gained 0.6% to 10,225 on Thursday, recouping some losses from the previous session, with artificial intelligence and related technology stocks leading the charge.

Those gains were driven by enthusiasm around Chinese artificial intelligence startup DeepSeek, which is poised to challenge Western AI leaders.

At the same time, concerns about a global trade war eased, as Beijing’s countermeasures against the 10% tariffs imposed by the Trump administration on Chinese goods have remained restrained.

The technology sector saw notable gains, led by 360 Security Technology (+4.4%), Fibocom Wireless (+11.5%), Talkweb Information (+5.9%), Naura Technology (+4.6%), and Goertek (+5%).

data.title: Vietnam Inflation Rate Climbs to 6-Month High
data.description:
The annual inflation rate in Vietnam rose to 3.63% in January 2025, the highest since July last year, up from 2.94% in December.

Main upward pressure came from a faster increase in prices for food and beverage services (4.42% vs. 3.87% in December) and health services (14.14% vs. 5.32%).

Inflation also edged higher for drinks and tobacco (2.72% vs. 2.40%), clothing and footwear (1.32% vs. 1.16%), home appliances (1.66% vs. 1.49%), and culture, entertainment, and tourism (2.48% vs. 2.31%).

In contrast, price growth slowed for housing and construction materials (4.95% vs. 5.16%).

Meanwhile, the annual core inflation rate, which excludes volatile items, rose to 3.07%—the highest since November 2023—up from 2.85% in December.

On a monthly basis, consumer prices grew 0.98%, following a 0.29% increase in the preceding period.

data.title: Nissin Foods' Profit Down 11% in Fiscal Nine Months; Shares Fall 3%
data.description:
Nissin Foods Holdings' profit attributable to owners of the parent declined 11.2% to 43.6 billion yen in the fiscal nine months ended Dec. 31, 2024, from 49.1 billion yen a year ago due to higher costs and expenses.

The food company said earnings per share fell to 144.40 yen from 160.32 yen a year ago, according to a Tokyo Stock Exchange filing on Wednesday.

Revenue grew 6.1% to 582.3 billion yen from 548.9 billion yen a year earlier.

Nissin Foods forecasted attributable profit to range between 54.50 billion yen and 57.50 billion yen, or between 179 yen and 189 yen per share, while net sales are expected to rise 7.1% to 785.00 billion yen for the fiscal year ending March 31.

The company expects to pay a year-end dividend of 35 yen per share, bringing the total annual dividend to 70 yen per share.

Shares of the company declined over 3% in late-morning trade Thursday.

data.title: Tamura says BoJ must raise short-term interest rates to at least 1% by the H2 fiscal 2025
data.description:
Bank of Japan board member Naoki Tamura spoke today:

BOJ's Tamura makes his case again for faster interest rate hikes

His comment on getting rates to 1% is catching headlines:

  • short-term interest rates shouldd be raised to at least 1% by the second half of fiscal 2025 to manage rising inflation risks

Tamura is known for his hawkish stance. He believes inflationary pressures are increasing, necessitating rate hikes to levels considered neutral for the economy.

  • expects Japan’s economy will be in a position by late 2025 to sustainably meet the BOJ’s 2% inflation target, supported by widespread wage growth, including among smaller firms
  • emphasised the need for gradual and timely rate increases as the likelihood of achieving the inflation goal grows

In January the BOJ raised interest rates to 0.5%, the highest level since the 2008 global financial crisis.

***

As a note, the Japanese fiscal year is from 1 April to 31 March. So I am taking a guess that H2 fiscal 2025 Tamura is referring to is some time after, say September, this year? That is still a sedate pace of rate hikes, no?

***

USD/JPY dropped to lose circa 151.80 on Tamura's remarks but has since bounced back a little:

data.title: Ethos Invests Additional INR20 Million in Wholly-Owned Arm
data.description:
Luxury watches retailer Ethos has invested an additional 20 million Indian rupees in its wholly-owned subsidiary RF Brands, according to a Wednesday filing to the Indian stock exchanges.

The company was allotted 2 million equity shares of 10 rupees each by way of subscription to the rights issue of RF Brands, a retailer of watches and other accessories.

data.title: South Korea's Current Account Surplus Rises in December 2024
data.description:
South Korea's current account surplus increased to $12.37 billion in December from $10.05 billion in the previous month, according to preliminary data from the Bank of Korea released Thursday.

Compared with the year-ago period, the figure rose from a surplus of $8.93 billion, the central bank said.

The goods account registered a surplus of $10.43 billion, driven by a 6.6% annual increase in exports to $63.3 billion. Imports also climbed 4.2% to $52.87 billion from the previous year.

Meanwhile, the services account posted a deficit of $2.11 billion, primarily due to deficits in manufacturing services, travel, and other business services accounts.

The primary income account recorded a $4.76 billion surplus in the month, owing to higher earnings from dividends, the data showed.

data.title: Japan Airport Terminal's Nine Month Profits Up Nearly 33% Amid Travel Boom
data.description:
Japan Airport Terminal's profit attributable to owners of the parent increased by 32.8% to 18.8 billion yen in the nine months ended Dec 31, 2024, up from 14.1 billion yen a year ago amid a surging number of travellers.

The Haneda airport operator said basic earnings per share (EPS) rose to 201.76 yen from 151.66 yen a year ago, according to a filing on Wednesday.

Operating revenue grew by 26.7% to 201.6 billion yen from more than 159 billion yen a year earlier.

For the full fiscal year ending March 31, the company forecasted attributable profit to grow slightly to 19.30 billion yen or 207.68 yen per share. Operating revenues are projected to increase by 22.9% to 267.3 billion yen.

The company expects to pay year-end dividend of 35 yen per share, bringing the total annual dividend to 70.00 yen per share for the current fiscal year.

Japan Airport Terminal's shares recently shed nearly 3%.

data.title: Market Chatter: South Korea Blocks Access to Chinese AI Service DeepSeek
data.description:
South Korea's foreign and trade ministries restricted access to the Chinese AI service DeepSeek, citing concerns over user data collection, Yonhap News reported Wednesday.

Ministry computers connected to external networks now display a message indicating that access is restricted when attempting to visit the site. The move highlights growing caution around data security issues linked to foreign AI platforms, the report said.

(Market Chatter news is derived from conversations with market professionals globally. This information is believed to be from reliable sources but may include rumor and speculation. Accuracy is not guaranteed.)

data.title: OneConnect Financial Chairman/CEO Resigns; Successor Named; Shares Fall 5%
data.description:
OneConnect Financial Technology said Chongfeng Shen resigned as chief executive officer and the board's chairman, according to a Wednesday filing with the Hong Kong bourse.

Chen Dangyang will succeed Chongfeng as the CEO and the board's chairman. Chen was previously the assistant president for Ping An Property and Casualty Insurance between August 2021 and February 2025.

All changes are effective Feb. 5, the filing noted.

OneConnect Financial shares dropped nearly 5% in morning trade Thursday.

data.title: Cybeats Closes Final Tranche of Non-Brokered Offering; Over $2M in Total Proceeds
data.description:
Toronto, Ontario--(Newsfile Corp. - February 5, 2025) - Cybeats Technologies Corp. (OTCQB: CYBCF) ("Cybeats'' or the "Company"), a global leader in software supply chain security, is pleased to announce the closing of its previously announced non-brokered private placement (the "Offering") for total gross proceeds of over $2 million. Proceeds from the Offering will be used to scale the Company's commercial presence, and support ongoing corporate activities.

Including the first tranche of the Offering, the Company has issued a total of 12,586,625 Units for total gross proceeds of $2,013,860. The Offering was completed in two tranches, with the second tranche issuing 2,177,875 units of the Company (each, a "Unit"), at a price of $0.16 per Unit, for gross proceeds of $348,460. Each Unit consists of one common share in the capital of the Company (each, a "Common Share") and one Common Share purchase warrant ("Warrant"). Each Warrant is exercisable into one Common Share at a price of $0.22 per Common Share for a period of 24 months from the date of issuance. All securities issued in connection with the Offering will be subject to a four month and one day hold as required under applicable securities laws.

This news release shall not constitute an offer to sell or the solicitation of an offer to buy securities in the United States, nor shall there be any sale of the securities in any jurisdiction in which such offer, solicitation or sale would be unlawful. The securities being offered have not been, nor will they be, registered under the United States Securities Act of 1933, as amended (the "1933 Act") or under any U.S. state securities laws, and may not be offered or sold in the United States absent registration or an applicable exemption from the registration requirements of the 1933 Act, as amended, and applicable state securities laws.

About Cybeats Technologies Corp.

Cybeats Technologies Corp. (OTCQB: CYBCF) is a cybersecurity company providing Software Bill of Material (SBOM) management and software supply chain intelligence technology, helping organizations to manage risk, meet compliance requirements, and secure their software from procurement to development and operation. Our platform gives customers comprehensive visibility and transparency into their software supply chain, enabling them to improve operational efficiency, increase revenue, and align organizations with current and future regulations. Cybeats. Software Made Certain. Website: https://cybeats.com

data.title: Teijin Sees FY Net Y25.00B
Teijin 9-Mos Net Y50.98B Vs Net Y10.70B
Seer CEO Omid Farokhzad Sells Shares to Cover Tax Obligations
data.description:
Reporter NameFarokhzad Omid
RelationshipCEO AND CHAIR
TypeSell
Amount$18,044
SEC FilingForm 4

Seer's CEO and Chair, Omid Farokhzad, sold 8,068 shares of Class A Common Stock on February 4, 2025, at a price of $2.2365 per share, totaling $18,044. This sale was conducted to satisfy tax obligations related to the vesting of restricted stock units. Following this transaction, Farokhzad directly owns 1,428,217 shares of Seer.

SEC Filing: Seer, Inc. [ SEER ] - Form 4 - Feb. 05, 2025

data.title: Fuji Media Holdings Profit Rises 25% in Fiscal Nine Months, Cuts Forecast; Shares Up 3%
data.description:
Fuji Media Holdings reported that its profit attributable to owners of the parent increased by 25.1% to 24.1 billion yen in the nine months ended Dec. 31, up from 19.3 billion yen a year ago.

The company said basic earnings per share (EPS) rose to 114.03 yen from 87.55 yen a year ago, according to a bourse filing on Wednesday.

Net sales grew by 0.8% to 413.3 billion yen from 410.1 billion yen a year earlier.

In a separate filing, the company cut its forecast for the full fiscal year ending March 31, due to advertisers suspending commercials on subsidiary Fuji TV following recent reports.

The network is replacing the advertisements with public service announcements without charging fees, leading to an expected decline in fourth-quarter advertising revenue and a projected drop in Fuji TV's non-consolidated net sales, operating income, recurring profit, and net income.

It forecasted attributable profit to decline to 9.80 billion yen from 29 billion yen previously or 46.60 yen per share from 137.27 yen per share. Net sales are projected to decrease to 548.20 billion yen from 598.3 billion yen previously projected.

The company declared a second-quarter dividend of 25.00 yen per share, payable to shareholders. It expects to pay an annual dividend of 50.00 yen per share for the current fiscal year.

Fuji Media Holdings' shares gained nearly 3% in early trading.

data.title: KLCC Property's 2025 Profit Could Improve on Better Tourism — Market Talk
data.description: KLCC Property Holdings' profit is expected to improve this year, driven by stronger tourism in Malaysia and full-year earnings contribution from its additional 40% stake in shopping mall Suria KLCC, Hong Leong IB says. Malaysia's Asean chairmanship in 2025 and the Visit Malaysia 2026 campaign could boost tourist arrivals, benefiting its hotel assets and Suria KLCC footfall, analyst Jack Heng Shao Jun says in a note. However, higher finance costs from the Suria KLCC acquisition could cap earnings growth, he says. Hong Leong maintains a hold rating on the REIT and keeps the target price at MYR7.71. Units are 0.4% higher at MYR8.38. (yingxian.wong@wsj.com)

data.title: Enova Mining reports high-grade titanium dioxide hits at CODA North in Brazil
data.description: Enova Mining has reported strong drilling results at its CODA North project in Brazil, with multiple intercepts exceeding 15% titanium dioxide, associated with rare earth element (REE) mineralisation. Drilling of 3,101 metres on ground within the Patos Formation returned 10 standout assays believed to underscore the project’s multi-commodity potential. The geochemical relationship suggests the possibility of co-extracting titanium alongside valuable REE, enhancing the overall project. Multi-resource extraction “Such findings position CODA North as a promising prospect for the development of multi-resource extraction strategies and support our mission to unlock high-value mineral opportunities in this region,” chief executive officer Eric Vesel said. “Our team has discovered significant REE potential at zones enriched with additional metals such as titanium, scandium and niobium, which could become by-products for a future rare earths operation,” Mr Vesel said. “We are excited about the opportunities this presents as we continue to advance our strategic growth initiatives.” Assay highlights Highlights from surface featured 54m at 9.03% titanium dioxide including 16m at 16.1% from 37m, 48m at 12.1% titanium dioxide including 14m at 19.2% from 21m and 37.4m at 12.26% titanium dioxide including 14m at 17.7% from 14m. Other notable results were 49m at 10.85% titanium dioxide including 16m at 15.4% from 11m, 49m at 10.5% titanium dioxide including 13m at 15.7% from 15m and 32m at 12.08% titanium dioxide from surface including 11m at 16.4% from 11m. Four more prospective REE mineralised zones await drilling—CODA East, CODA XN, CODA XS and CODA South. Versatile metal Titanium is a versatile metal known for its exceptional strength-to-weight ratio, corrosion resistance and high-temperature stability, making it essential across a range of industries. It is widely used in the aerospace and defence sectors for aircraft components and military equipment and in the automotive sector for the manufacture of lightweight and durable parts. Titanium’s biocompatibility makes it ideal for medical implants and devices, while its corrosion resistance supports applications in chemical processing, marine environments and desalination plants. The global titanium dioxide market was valued at $32.22 billion in 2023 and is expected to grow at a compound annual rate of 6.2% to $55.37b by 2032.

data.title: Thai baht/US dollar slightly weaker on Thursday
data.description:
The Thai baht weakened slightly against the U.S. dollar on Thursday.

At 0214 GMT, the baht was 0.09% lower at 33.58 per dollar, after trading in a range of 33.560 to 33.620. It ended the previous session at 33.55 per dollar, as per LSEG data.

data.title: New Zealand's Contact Energy gains on Manawa deal update
data.description:
** Contact Energy rises 1.1% to A$8.390

** Stock rose as much as 5.7% to A$8.791 earlier in the session, highest since Jan. 20

** Energy retailer says it acknowledges Statement of Issues released by the New Zealand Commerce Commission, assessing the competitive effects of co buying Manawa Energy

** Adds both firms provided substantive evidence and will continue to assist the Commission in matters noted in the statement

** Statement says merger will raise potential competition issues

** YTD, CEN down 2.1%

data.title: Nissan CEO Uchida tells Honda CEO Mibe he wants to terminate merger talks, Asahi reports
data.description: Nissan Motor CEO Makoto Uchida told his Honda counterpart Toshihiro Mibe that he wants to terminate their discussions over a possible merger, the Asahi newspaper reported online on Thursday.

data.title: China's ClouDr rises to 4-month high on medical AI integration with DeepSeek-R1 model
data.description:
** Shares of Chinese medical services provider ClouDr Group Ltd jump as much as 27.3% to HK$2.05, their highest since October 8, and on course for a third straight session of gains

** Stock set for the biggest one-day pct rise since October 2, 2024, if gains hold

** The provider of medical supplies and software-as-a-service (SaaS) products to hospitals and pharmacies announces the integration of its medical artificial intelligence (AI) ClouDr Brain with DeepSeek-R1 model

** Says the integration will enhance efficiency of chronic condition management of its hospital and pharmacy SaaS, and will strengthen data mining capability of its ClouDr Brain and accelerating ClouDr Brain's application in chronic condition management

** Hang Seng Tech Index adds 0.3%, while Hang Seng Commerce & Industry Index (.HSNC) and Hang Seng Index both climb 0.1% each

** ClouDr Group stock lost 82.5% last year

data.title: Vietnam Retail Sales Growth at 8-Month High
data.description:
Retail sales in Vietnam advanced by 9.5% year-on-year in January 2025 from a 9.3% increase in the previous month.

This marked the 38th consecutive month of growth in retail activity and the strongest since May 2024, as sales accelerated for accommodation & food services (14.8% vs 13.1% in December), tourism activity (17.3% vs 9.9%), and other services (9.8% vs 7.9%).

On the other hand, trade growth slowed for goods (8.6% vs 9%).

On a monthly basis, retail activity grew by 2.7%.

data.title: Dollar Weakens as Trade Concerns Fade
data.description:
The dollar index dipped to around 107.5 on Thursday, extending its decline for a fourth consecutive session as concerns over a global trade war eased following cautious tariff measures from the US and China.

US President Donald Trump and Chinese President Xi Jinping are also expected to discuss trade developments in an upcoming call, further reducing the risk of rising inflation.

This shift in sentiment has led markets to maintain expectations of two rate cuts by the Federal Reserve this year.

Traders have fully priced in a 25-basis-point cut in July, with another quarter-point reduction anticipated before the year ends.

Meanwhile, data released on Wednesday revealed that the US economy added over 180,000 private-sector jobs in January, surpassing the forecast of 150,000 and signaling resilience despite ongoing economic uncertainty.

While the dollar weakened against most currencies, it continued to depreciate against the yen, partly driven by hawkish comments from Japanese officials.

data.title: Yamaha's Profit Down 31% in April-December 2024 Period; Shares Gain Nearly 6%
data.description:
Yamaha's profit attributable to owners of the parent declined 31.0% to 14.3 billion yen in the fiscal nine months ended Dec. 31, 2024, from 20.7 billion yen a year ago mainly due to impairment losses recognized on the company's production facilities in Indonesia.

The company said basic earnings per share (EPS) fell to 29.13 yen from 40.84 yen a year ago, according to a Tokyo Stock Exchange filing on Wednesday.

Revenue increased 2.6% to 350.7 billion yen from 341.8 billion yen a year earlier.

For the fiscal year ending March 31, the company expects attributable profit to decline 54.5% to 13.50 billion yen or 27.66 yen per share, while net sales are expected to edge down 0.6% to 460 billion yen.

The company expects to pay a year-end dividend of 13 yen per share, bringing the total annual dividend to 50 yen per share on a post-stock split basis.

Yamaha's shares gained nearly 6% in early trading.
